title: rhus javanica var. roxburghii
intro: Rhus javanica var. roxburghii, also known as the Indian staff tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Rhus javanica var. roxburghii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and topical creams for medicinal purposes.
